audit_items.proof_trace: ctx allocated by damon_new_ctx() at L434 is not freed when damon_select_ops() fails at L438-439: the function returns -EINVAL directly without calling damon_destroy_ctx(ctx). The later damon_new_target() failure at L446 correctly calls damon_destroy_ctx(ctx), confirming developer awareness of the cleanup pattern, but the earlier path is missing it. No module_exit or deferred cleanup exists to recover the allocation.
audit_items.attack_scenario: If DAMON_OPS_PADDR is not registered at module load time (e.g., dependency ordering issue or kernel built without paddr ops support), damon_select_ops() returns -EINVAL and the struct damon_ctx allocation from damon_new_ctx() is permanently leaked. This is a one-time kernel memory leak at module initialization.
